Clinical Reasoning:
According to the detailed description given in "[article-29382_1]", the pulp proper of the spleen comprises mainly white blood cells involved in initiating the adaptive immune response. Specifically, it mentions that the germinal center region within the white pulp houses mostly B-cells, while regions like the marginal zone house T-cells. Given that the pulp proper refers here to the white pulp, we should focus on what is described there regarding cellular composition.
Answer: \boxed{C} Based on the information stating that B-cells populate the germinal centers and parts of the white pulp in the spleen, and considering that lymphocytes in the pulp proper align closely with the distribution patterns outlined,
